subject: ali-yahya

sectors_excited.name: AI × crypto (decentralized & verifiable AI)
sectors_excited.why: AI centralizes power by default; crypto is its counterweight. The near-term unlock is verifiable compute — letting AI models and agents run with cryptographic guarantees of correct, credibly-neutral execution — plus decentralized markets for the compute, data, and models that today only tech giants control. This is the design space I keep returning to (Gensyn for decentralized training, EigenCloud as the verifiable cloud for agents).

citations.quote: Crypto and AI are intersecting to create a design space that we've barely begun to explore.
AI needs verifiable computing to build trust. @eigencloud has the answer

sectors_excited.name: Verifiable computing / restaking (EigenCloud)
sectors_excited.why: Restaking unbundles cryptoeconomic security from the EVM, so builders can extend Ethereum — new VMs, oracles, bridges, specialized hardware — without spinning up a chain. Same 100x unlock that smart contracts were for application logic, and now the substrate for verifiable AI and agentic finance.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges, and networks with specialized hardware
the Eigen team has unlocked a whole new paradigm for building applications that are verifiable and credibly neutral

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge & privacy
sectors_excited.why: Privacy isn't just user protection — it unlocks a whole new region of the design space: compliant private payments, identity that proves without leaking, games with hidden state, shared private state for institutions. ZK proofs and newer secure-hardware approaches are finally making private-yet-verifiable blockchains real. I've backed this across Aleo, Iron Fish, Aztec, Nym, and Seismic.

citations.quote: Zero Knowledge Proofs will completely transform the web3 tech stack in the coming years
Privacy is important for web3, not just to protect user data, but because it unlocks a whole new region of the design space

sectors_excited.name: Cross-chain interoperability & composability
sectors_excited.why: The multichain future is settled; the open problem is making it feel like one computer again. Seamless composability is what makes onchain apps powerful, and fragmentation is the tax we pay for scale. Interop layers — LayerZero for messaging, Espresso for shared sequencing, Universal for bringing any asset to any chain — are how we win the network effects back.

citations.quote: There is no longer any question that the future of crypto and web3 is multi-chain. @LayerZero_Labs has built critical infrastructure that makes that possible.
In order for crypto to play a larger role in global finance, we need a way to bring any asset onto any chain in an inexpensive, verifiable, and composable way.

sectors_excited.name: Stablecoins & onchain payments
sectors_excited.why: Stablecoins are crypto's clearest product-market fit — fast, global, programmable money — and the rails for recurring, automated, agent-driven commerce are now being built (Loop, Universal). This is where crypto most obviously plugs into the real financial system.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are turbocharging online payments: fast, global, programmable.

sectors_excited.name: DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure)
sectors_excited.why: Crypto incentives can bootstrap real-world networks bottom-up that previously only telco/utility giants could build — wireless (Helium), energy/DERs (Daylight), compute (Gensyn). It's the strongest proof that crypto's usefulness goes well beyond currency.

citations.quote: It's a perfect example of how crypto applications go well beyond crypto-currencies.

sectors_concerned.name: The speculative casino / memecoins-as-an-end
sectors_concerned.why: My one persistent worry is cultural, not technical: crypto used as a degenerate casino — memecoins, 100x perps, airdrop farming — instead of for long-term alignment and real coordination. The casino doesn't bootstrap the space; it's the primary force holding it back and the thing that discredits everything real we're building.
sectors_concerned.age_note: stated 2023-12, a continuing conviction, not restated in my recent record

citations.quote: Or, you can use it to build a degenerate casino of meaningless speculation that turns everything into a zero sum game.
we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff

sectors_concerned.name: Ecosystem fragmentation (siloed chains & rollups)
sectors_concerned.why: The flip side of the rollup-centric roadmap: thousands of siloed chains and L2s can fragment liquidity and network effects. Modularity that extends network effects is great; modularity that shatters them is value-destructive. It's a real risk we have to engineer around — which is exactly why I'm spending so much time on interop.

citations.quote: only two kinds of modularity — modularity that extends and strengthens network effects; and modularity that fragments and weakens them
The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.

tokens_excited.name: ZRO (LayerZero)
tokens_excited.why: I put real, long-term conviction behind this — an additional $55M with a 3-year lockup in April 2025. It's the messaging/interop layer for an onchain financial system that's actually moving real workflows on-chain. I hold it as infrastructure, not a trade.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup

tokens_excited.name: EIGEN (EigenCloud)
tokens_excited.why: Same posture — an additional $70M with a lockup in June 2025. EigenCloud is becoming the verifiable cloud: the substrate for credibly-neutral, verifiable applications, including the AI/agent use cases I care most about.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup

tokens_excited.name: ETH (Ethereum)
tokens_excited.why: I think of ETH as ownership in the most secure programmable blockchain and the bedrock of security for the whole rollup ecosystem — a platform bet for the long arc, not a price call. Post-Merge it's more secure, fair, and efficient, and restaking/rollups keep extending its reach.
tokens_excited.age_note: platform conviction stated across 2022-2024; I treat it as a computer, not an asset, and don't make price calls

citations.quote: the most secure programmable blockchain: Ethereum

tokens_excited.name: SOL (Solana)
tokens_excited.why: Conviction in Solana as a platform dates to leading their round in 2021 — highest-performance L1 that preserves composability, with a builder culture I admire. Same framing: I'm betting on the ecosystem for the long term, not trading the token.
tokens_excited.age_note: stated 2021-06 when we led Solana's round; sustained ecosystem conviction, not a recent price view

citations.quote: @solana is a next-generation blockchain that offers the highest level of performance while preserving some of the most important features of Ethereum, like seamless program composability

tokens_concerned.name: Memecoins (as a category)
tokens_concerned.why: Tokens with no soul or purpose that moon on memes and then fall off a cliff. They aren't funding networks or aligning anyone — they're extractive and zero-sum. I have no view on any specific memecoin's price; I just think the category is bad for the space.
tokens_concerned.age_note: stated 2023-12, an enduring view, not restated in my recent record

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff

risk_regime.why: I genuinely don't operate in risk-on/risk-off terms — I under-index on price and the cycle on purpose. My posture is structural: long-term conviction in the technology. Prices fluctuate every cycle; innovation compounds through all of them. So I don't take a tactical market-timing position — I back builders and hold core positions with multi-year lockups.

notes: How to read this: I'm a builder-investor who steers toward technology and design spaces, not token prices. My 'tokens' views are infrastructure/platform conviction (often held with lockups), not trading calls — the honest center of gravity here is the sectors, not the tickers. The current tailwind is a far more constructive U.S. regulatory environment, but my excitement is about what's now buildable: verifiable AI, private-yet-verifiable computation, and interop that re-knits a fragmented multichain world into something that again feels like one computer. None of this is investment advice.
